2606.30602.title: MESA: Prioritizing Vulnerable Communication Channels for Securing Multi-Agent Systems
2606.30602.paper_summary: Multi-agent systems (MAS) are increasingly used to automate complex, distributed workflows. However, their inter-agent communication channels introduce new attack surfaces that remain poorly understood and are difficult to defend against. In this paper, we address how defenders should prioritize limited security effort to protect vulnerable communication channels before attacks are observed. This is motivated by our observation that the channel-level attack impact is highly non-uniform: a single compromised edge can account for up to 75% of total attack success. We introduce Mesa, a label-free framework for proactively ranking which MAS edges are most security-critical -- that is, most likely to affect the system's decision if compromised. Mesa combines six graph-theoretic metrics and two dynamic probes (ablation and masking) without requiring attack traces. We evaluate Mesa against a dynamic misinformation attack pipeline across three diverse MAS scenarios, eight network topologies, and five open-source LLMs from Qwen, Llama, and Gemma families. Mesa rankings correlate strongly with empirical per-edge attack success rate, achieving mean Spearman $ρ=+0.60$ (peaking at $+0.73$). In resource-constrained defense deployment, monitoring the top 10% of Mesa-ranked edges intercepts about 3x the successful attacks as random allocation. We further test Mesa under varying attacker and defender models and LangGraph workflows and characterize its limits under adaptive attacks and high-redundancy graphs. Overall, our results show that edge-level risk in MAS is often concentrated and predictable, allowing proactive hardening of multi-agent infrastructures.
2606.30602.paper_summary_zh: 多智能體系統（MAS）越來越多地被用來自動化複雜的分散工作流程。<lb/>然而，它們之間的通信通道引入了新的攻擊面，這些攻擊面仍然不太明瞭，且難以防禦。<lb/>在本文中，我們探討了防禦者應如何優先考慮有限的安全工作，以保護脆弱的通信通道，直到攻擊被觀察到。<lb/>這一點的動機源於我們的觀察，即通道級別的攻擊影響是高度不均勻的：單一被攻陷的邊緣可能佔總攻擊成功率的高達75%。<lb/>我們介紹了Mesa，一個無標籤的框架，用於主動排名哪些MAS邊緣是最具安全關鍵性的——也就是說，如果被攻陷，最有可能影響系統決策的邊緣。<lb/>Mesa結合了六個圖論指標和兩個動態探測（消融和遮蔽），而不需要攻擊痕跡。<lb/>我們在三個不同的MAS場景、八種網絡拓撲和來自Qwen、Llama和Gemma家族的五個開源LLM上評估了Mesa，針對一個動態虛假信息攻擊管道。<lb/>Mesa的排名與每個邊緣的實證攻擊成功率強烈相關，達到平均Spearman $ρ=+0.60$（峰值為$+0.73$）。<lb/>在資源受限的防禦部署中，監控Mesa排名前10%的邊緣攔截的成功攻擊約為隨機分配的3倍。<lb/>我們進一步在不同的攻擊者和防禦者模型以及LangGraph工作流程下測試Mesa，並描述其在自適應攻擊和高冗餘圖中的限制。<lb/>總體而言，我們的結果顯示，MAS中的邊緣級風險往往是集中且可預測的，這使得多智能體基礎設施的主動加固成為可能。
2606.30602.author: Kunyang Li et.al.
2606.30602.authors: Kunyang Li, Kyle Domico, Jonathan Gregory, Patrick McDaniel
